home.heroDescription: You know you need AI. Together, we'll find where to start, what makes the most sense for your business, and launch the first useful solution.
home.painTitle: AI only makes sense when it solves a real problem in your business.
home.pain1Description: There are many options. Before choosing a tool, we need to name the business problem worth solving first.
home.pain2Description: AI agents cannot become another experiment without clear results. Up front, we define how much time they should save, which costs they should reduce, or exactly what value they should create for customers.
home.pain3Description: Data access, rules, permissions, and gradual rollout matter as much as the tool itself. Your team needs an AI solution they understand and can use safely.
home.pain3AtlasText: See the public Agent Threat Atlas
home.atlasLandingTitle: Turn incident patterns into controls before your agents ship.
home.atlasLandingDescription: I help teams map agent workflows, data access, tool permissions, and failure modes into practical guardrails they can own in production.
home.consultingDescription: If you are not sure where to start, I will help map opportunities, choose the right first project, and decide how we will measure success.
home.implementationDescription: If you already have a clear problem, I will design and build a practical AI solution that fits your processes, tools, and security rules.
home.trainingDescription: If AI is going to work long term, your team needs to use it in everyday work. I help people find concrete use cases, verify outputs, and build safe work habits.

processSteps.title: Introductory Call
processSteps.description: 30 minutes, no commitment. We clarify where you are, what you want to improve, and whether AI is the right next step.

processSteps.title: First-Step Plan
processSteps.description: I propose a concrete first use case, a success metric, safety rules, and a realistic rollout path.

processSteps.title: Execution
processSteps.description: We build the approved workflow, test it on real tasks, train the first users, and measure its impact.

processSteps.title: Handoff
processSteps.description: Once the solution works, your team takes it over. If you want to keep going, long-term support remains available.

firstStepOffer.title: AI Opportunity and Risk Audit
firstStepOffer.description: In 1–2 weeks we map where AI makes sense in your company, which workflow to tackle first, what risks need controls, and how success will be measured.

home.atlasProofTitle: AI agent security is not an abstract risk.
home.atlasProofDescription: I maintain a public atlas of agentic AI incidents that maps real failures to threat categories and prevention principles teams can use before deployment.
home.trustDescription: I recommend the technology and rollout path that fit your business problem, team, budget, and safety needs.
home.ctaBandTitle: Ready to find your first useful AI project?
home.ctaBandDescription: Most clients start with a 30-minute consultation. No commitment, no unnecessary hype.

services.heroDescription: From the first AI use case to safe deployment.

notSure.title: Not sure where to start?
notSure.description: Most clients start with a 30-minute consultation. No commitment, no jargon — just an honest conversation about where AI might create measurable value.

about.heroDescription: I help companies turn AI pressure into safe, measurable solutions.
about.bioText:
I am an agentic AI consultant with 10 years of software engineering experience. I help small and medium-sized businesses find specific places where AI can save time, streamline teamwork, and create measurable value.

I enter projects practically: first I understand the business problems, processes, and data sources, then I choose the right tools. Together we design the first usable workflow, set success metrics, and define safe deployment rules.

My solutions build on experience with LLM systems, RAG, evaluations, AI models, automations, and AI agents. My goal is to deliver a solution leadership understands, the team can use, and the company can keep developing independently.
about.principle1Description: Open source or managed, self-hosted or cloud — I recommend what fits your team, budget, and security requirements.
about.principle2Description: I train your team alongside deployment. The goal is for you to own your AI workflows and keep developing them.
about.principle3Description: First we validate one useful use case. We measure results and expand only what works.
about.techDescription: The right technical solution depends on the workflow. Sometimes a lightweight automation is enough; other times it takes a RAG system or a custom AI agent connected to existing tools. What they share are clear rules, controlled access, measurable results, and a team that knows how to use the solution.
about.ctaTitle: Let's talk about the first AI workflow worth solving.

contact.bookDescription: Choose a time that works for you.

faq.question: We don't know where to start with AI. Is it worth getting in touch?
faq.answer: Yes. This is the most common starting point. On the first call we look at your processes, data, and the places where the team loses time. The goal is a clear first use case, not a list of tools.

faq.question: How long does the first deployment take?
faq.answer: Most first projects move from kickoff to a working workflow in 2–6 weeks. A simple automation can be live in days. More complex systems take longer, but you should see progress every week.

faq.question: How do we know whether AI is creating real value?
faq.answer: Before building, we define the metric: time saved, shorter cycle time, fewer errors, better quality, or faster customer response. If value cannot be measured, we narrow the project or postpone it.

faq.question: How do you handle data security?
faq.answer: We start with data, permissions, and deployment boundaries. The first version should use only the minimum necessary access, clear rules for sensitive data, and human review where the risk is higher. With security, slower and steadier is better.

faq.question: Do we need our own technical team?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. I can handle the technical side. Someone technical on your team helps with long-term maintenance and ownership, but the first project can start without an internal developer.

faq.question: What tools do you work with?
faq.answer: Tools depend on the problem, data, and security requirements. For workflow automation I use n8n, CrewAI, or LangChain/LangGraph. For models I choose frontier models from OpenAI and Anthropic, or open models such as Mistral and Llama. For commercial agentic tools, Codex and Claude Code can make sense. If you need more control or self-hosting, we can also work with open-source agents such as OpenClaw or Hermes Agent. The goal is not to use as many tools as possible, but to choose a stack that is safe, maintainable, and creates measurable value.

faq.question: What happens after handoff?
faq.answer: Once the workflow works, your team gets the operating rules, training, and enough context to use it safely. The goal is capability, not dependency. If useful, we can keep improving the workflow together.

footer.description: Agentic AI for small and medium-sized businesses where measurability and security matter.
